# Journal: 2026-04-14

## Summary

48-hour shadow validation completed with zero discrepancies. Phase 4 10% production traffic shift deployed. Waiting for 24h hold.

## Work Done

- Shadow mode validation (Phase 3) completed at 2026-04-15 14:00 UTC.
- Total diff checks: 576 (every 5 minutes over 48 hours). Zero discrepancies. Phase 3 PASSED.
- @sre-lead reviewed Phase 3 results and approved Phase 4 deployment.
- Deployed Phase 4 first step: 10% production traffic shifted to new topology.
- Monitored Phase 4 10% for 4 hours:
  - Consumer lag: stable at ~150 messages per partition (well under 10K alarm threshold).
  - End-to-end latency P99: 2.3 minutes (well under 5-minute SLA).
  - DLQ rate: 0.002% (within acceptable range, all records are known edge cases).
  - No backpressure events triggered.
- Updated Grafana dashboard to show new topology metrics alongside old.
- Fixed integration test flakiness: added consumer group cleanup to EmbeddedKafka test fixture.
- Pushed cleanup PR — merged same day.
- Followed up with data governance on DG-0041 — response expected by 2026-04-17.
- Followed up with infra on INF-0892 — ETA 2026-04-18.

## Decisions

- 24-hour hold at 10% before moving to 50% (per migration plan, approved by @sre-lead).
- DLQ rate 0.002% deemed acceptable — records are pre-existing malformed events in test traffic.
- Proceed to 50% shift on 2026-04-15 after confirming 24-hour metrics stable.
- If any alarm fires during 24h hold, revert immediately to 0% and investigate.

## Blockers

- 50% traffic shift blocked until 2026-04-15 (24h hold requirement — not a problem blocker).
- DG-0041 still outstanding — DLQ topic can operate without formal approval but audit risk exists.
- INF-0892 outstanding — Grafana new metric namespaces not yet provisioned.
- Downstream consumer nestads-reporting-service (WP-4199) still not merged — statistics format change not yet live in reporting.

## Next

- Monitor Phase 4 10% hold for 24 hours (until 2026-04-15 ~14:00 UTC).
- Deploy Phase 4 50% shift on 2026-04-15 if 24h metrics stable.
- Follow up on DG-0041 and INF-0892.
- Begin drafting runbook update for incident response.
- Start decommission checklist for old topology (for Phase 5).

## Notes

- Phase 4 deployment runbook: https://wiki.nestads.internal/runbooks/statistics-stream-phase4.
- Consumer lag alarm threshold: 10,000 messages per partition (Grafana alert ID: alrt-0892).
- Latency P99 alarm: 5 minutes end-to-end (Grafana alert ID: alrt-0893).
